name: Sumba (Tarimbang, Occy's Left / Nihiwatu) / 松巴島 / Pulau Sumba
gps_location: Nihiwatu / Occy's Left: Wanukaka, southwest Sumba, approx. -9.78, 119.36 (Nihi Sumba resort area). Tarimbang Bay / Miller's Right: southern coast East Sumba, approx. -10.20, 120.42 [uncertain exact coords]
wave_type: Two flagship breaks: Occy's Left / Nihiwatu - long left-hand reef break / point break, performance walls and barrels up to 300m. Tarimbang (Miller's Right) - right-hand reef break with multiple sections, long playful walls with some hollow sections, plus exposed lefts in the bay.
skill_level: Occy's Left approachable to all surfers below overhead, demanding/advanced at size. Miller's Right intermediate to advanced. Tarimbang exposed lefts more demanding.
wave_height_range: 3 ft to many times overhead (~3-15+ ft). Occy's Left works from 3ft up. Miller's Right best on small to medium days (3-8 ft).
bottom_type: reef (coral; Sumba also features dead coral, volcanic rock, and boulders on some breaks)
best_season: Dry season May-October; peak July-September for biggest cleanest swells. Shoulder months (April, October) good balance of swell and lighter winds.
best_tide: Occy's Left works most tides; mid to high more forgiving, low tide more hollow and shallow. Miller's Right varies by section.
best_wind: Offshore E to SE trade winds (dry season); can be cross-shore on certain breaks at peak SE trade strength.
swell_consistency_rating: 4 (consistent dry-season groundswells; Sumba sits exposed to Indian Ocean Roaring 40s swells)
wave_length_meters: Occy's Left up to 300m; Miller's Right one of longest rights in Sumba on its day (~150-250m) [uncertain exact length]
wave_speed_rating: medium to fast (Occy's Left has powerful lips and fast inside bowl; Miller's Right more medium / playful)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Occy's Left has steep drops on size with shallow reef inside bowl; late takeoffs hit reef. Miller's Right easier takeoff with multiple sections.
secondary_peaks: Sumba has additional breaks - Pinnacles (heavy reef), Tarimbang lefts (exposed), Miller's Right and inside sections, plus more remote breaks accessible by 4WD or boat. Multiple bays scattered along south coast.
peak_crowd_level: 1 (extremely uncrowded; Occy's Left capped at 10-12 surfers/day resort-only; Tarimbang sees only handful of surfers at a time)
access: Bali (DPS) -> Tambolaka (TMC) ~1hr flight, up to 3x daily, for Nihiwatu/west Sumba; or Bali -> Waingapu (WGP) for east Sumba/Tarimbang. Nihiwatu: 1.5hr scenic drive from Tambolaka through highlands. Tarimbang: ~2hr drive from Waingapu airport, then 15-min walk or 20-min paddle from Marthens Homestay to the surf break.
paddle_out_method: Occy's Left: resort crew guides guests via safe paddle-out channel from beachfront - no public access allowed. Miller's Right: walk along beach then paddle from inside reef; channel access on north side of bay.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol and morning sessions preferred for cleanest conditions; SE trades can build during day affecting some breaks.
boat_charter_required: Not required - both Nihiwatu (resort guest beach access) and Tarimbang (land access via homestay) reachable by land. Some remote Sumba breaks require 4WD or boat.
combo_trip_potential: Excellent combo with Bali, Rote, and broader Nusa Tenggara island-hopping. Some private/luxury charters include Sumba in extended Indonesian itineraries.
hazards: Shallow reef, powerful lips at Occy's Left, remote location with limited medical, strong trade winds, exposed coastline with strong rip currents, deep coral on inside bowls
marine_hazards: Sharp coral reef cuts (primary), sea urchins, occasional jellyfish, stonefish in reef pools, sharks rare but possible in open-ocean exposed coast
lineup_etiquette: Occy's Left strictly managed by resort surf concierge with rotation/priority system among 10-12 daily surfers. Miller's Right and other breaks: respect locals and other guests; standard wave priority.
localism_intensity: low (Sumba has very few local surfers; Tarimbang locals are welcoming; Occy's Left has no public locals since it is private resort wave)
respect_protocol: Respect strict Nihi resort surf protocols (booking required, max 1 surfer per room, no outside paddle-ins). At Tarimbang, support Marthens Homestay and local community; respect Sumba's strong adat (traditional) culture including ikat weaving villages, megalithic tombs, and animist-Christian customs.
medical_nearby: Nihiwatu has on-site paramedic + International SOS phone access; next hospital ~30min in Waikabubak; medevac available from Tambolaka (~70min drive) to Bali or Singapore. Tarimbang very remote - small village clinic only; Waingapu (~2hr) has district hospital. Serious cases require medevac.
rescue_jet_ski_available: Nihiwatu has resort water-safety crew with boats and guides on lineup; no standing jet ski public rescue at Tarimbang [uncertain on jet ski specifically]
emergency_contact: Nihi resort 24/7 emergency line via International SOS; Indonesia general 112; insurance medevac line essential [uncertain specific numbers]
accommodation_options: Nihiwatu (Nihi Sumba Island) - ultra-luxury resort, all-inclusive from approx USD 2,365-2,783+/night with 3-night minimum, surf capped at 10-12 guests/day. Tarimbang: Marthens Homestay (basic bamboo bungalows IDR 400k-800k/night including 3 meals), Sumba Surf Camp, plus a small number of other homestays. Tarimbang Beach also has limited mid-range options. Maringi Eco-Resort and other emerging boutique options elsewhere on island.
webcam_url: No reliable public live webcam for Sumba surf spots (Nihiwatu is private; Tarimbang remote) [uncertain]
annual_competition_window: No public WSL competitions; Nihi Sumba hosts private invitational surf events and coaching weeks with pro surfers [uncertain dates]
community_events: No regular weekly community surf events; Sumba hosts cultural events like Pasola (ritual horse jousting) Feb-Mar in west Sumba which can coincide with surf trips [uncertain surf-specific]
